subject: Physics
title: Gravitational Lensing
description: A fast breakdown of Gravitational Lensing for builders.

hook.text: Massive objects bend light around them like a lens. Einstein predicted it; astronomers now use it to see the invisible.

buildup.text: Einstein's general relativity says mass curves spacetime. Light follows that curvature.

discovery.text: When a massive galaxy sits between us and a distant object, it bends the light into arcs and rings.

twist.text: Astronomers use lensing to map dark matter. It bends light even though it's invisible.

climax.text: Gravitational lensing has revealed galaxies so distant their light has traveled 13 billion years to reach us.

punchline.text: Gravity bends light. The universe becomes its own telescope.

quiz.question: How is gravitational lensing used to study dark matter?
options: Dark matter bends light from distant objects, revealing its location and mass
Dark matter absorbs light, creating shadows
Dark matter emits its own faint light
